title: Day 57: Fractura Total Day 9 — CJNG 'Monstruos' Armored Convoy Patrols Jalisco-Zacatecas Border; ACLED Webinar Set for April 23
detail: Day 57 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). The CJNG fractura total — the open internal war between the El Jardinero/El Sapo faction and O3/Los Cuinis (declared April 11) — enters Day 9 of declared conflict with no direct inter-faction combat confirmed in the CJNG Jalisco heartland, consistent with an ongoing dual-buildup phase. The week's most significant field development: on Saturday evening, April 18, a CJNG armed convoy deploying 'Monstruos' (improvised armored vehicles reinforced with steel plating) was reported in the Colotlán municipality area, near the Jalisco-Zacatecas state border. The convoy, comprising Monstruos and at least two additional vehicles, circulated across multiple border-zone municipalities: Colotlán, Chimaltitán, Totatitla, Bolaños, Villa Guerrero, and Santa María de los Ángeles. An armed clash was reported at approximately 7:20 PM local time in Laurel, Tepetongo municipality (Zacatecas), where the convoy crossed from Jalisco into Zacatecas. No confirmed casualties or arrests were reported at publication time. The Colotlán-Tepetongo corridor sits within the northern perimeter of El Jardinero's established Jalisco and Zacatecas plaza network — the deployment of Monstruos-class vehicles, previously associated with CJNG's paramilitary posture in the Zacatecas-Jalisco war zone, is consistent with territorial marking and pre-combat positioning rather than opportunistic tactical engagement. On Day 9 of the fractura total: O3 retains Grupo Élite paramilitary command and Los Cuinis financial infrastructure; El Jardinero controls multi-state plazas across Jalisco, Nayarit, Zacatecas, Michoacán, and Guerrero; El Sapo manages Puerto Vallarta port logistics and Chinese fentanyl precursor supply chains. Both factions are assessed to be identifying territorial vulnerabilities before committing to direct confrontation in the Jalisco heartland or Colima. Analytical community milestone approaching: ACLED's live webinar 'After El Mencho: CJNG Succession Dynamics and the Future of Security in Mexico' is scheduled for April 23 — the first major international analytical event examining post-Mencho succession dynamics, featuring InSight Crime's Chris Dalby, Victoria Dittmar, and Sandra Pellegrini. FIFA World Cup 2026: 52 days to the opening match (June 11, Mexico City). Guadalajara's Akron Stadium hosts four group-stage matches; Mexico's security posture remains elevated at all three host cities.

media.caption: CJNG 'Monstruos' armored vehicles deployed on Jalisco-Zacatecas border near Colotlán, April 18, 2026
